engineCodes.h2: Aston Martin Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Aston Martin engine — VH-era V12 and V8, plus modern Mercedes-AMG units in DB11 and Vantage. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Aston Martin specialists.

groups.name: Modern Mercedes-AMG Engines
groups.failureNote: Get Quote → Common failure points (expandable note): M177 engines are known for intake valve deposits and carbon buildup due to direct injection — our rebuilt units include catch can systems and decarbonised cylinder heads . AE31 V12 units require specialist timing chain and cooling system attention; all our V12 rebuilds include upgraded chain guides and water pump inspection.

groups.name: VH-era V12 Engines
groups.failureNote: Developer note: The AM04 and AM08 are Cosworth-derived units fitted to DB9, DBS, Vanquish, and Rapide. Get Quote → Common failure points (expandable note): AM04/AM08 V12 engines suffer coolant leaks from ageing liner seals — our rebuilds include full liner seal replacement and upgraded 'O' rings . Ignition coil failures on early units are resolved with uprated coil packs.

groups.name: VH-era V8 Engines
groups.failureNote: Get Quote → Common failure points (expandable note): AJ-V8 engines are known for camshaft wear and timing chain stretch — our rebuilt units include new timing components and revised oil jets. Routine inspection of valve seats prevents catastrophic valve failure on higher-mileage units.

engines.cta: Get Quote for AJ-V8 Gen III
Get Quote for AJ-V8 Gen IV

groups.name: Heritage Straight-Six and V8 Engines
groups.failureNote: Get Quote → Common failure points (expandable note): Heritage six‑cylinder engines suffer from cylinder liner seal failures leading to water ingress into oil — our rebuilds include correctly seated liners and upgraded gaskets . The 5.3L V8 requires liner seal replacement and corrosion treatment; all our units are pressure-tested before dispatch. Not sure which engine code your Aston Martin uses? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine and best quote — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Aston Martin specialists.

commonProblems.h2: Common Aston Martin Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Aston Martin’s V8 and V12 engines deliver exceptional performance, but when they fail—typically due to timing chain stretch, core plug corrosion, or bearing wear—repair bills can quickly reach 2–3x those of mainstream brands. The DB9 and Vantage families dominate replacement demand, with many owners facing a genuine rebuild vs replace decision around 80,000–120,000 miles. Every rebuilt Aston Martin engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point—plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the four most common failure patterns, with honest cost comparisons between main dealers and independent Aston Martin engine specialists.

problems.h4: Timing Chain & Tensioner Failure – The VH-Generation V12 Weakness
problems.rootCause: The original timing chain guides and tensioners use plastic components that become brittle with heat cycling. When a guide fractures, the chain slackens, jumps timing, and allows pistons to contact valves. The chain itself also stretches over time, exacerbating the problem.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Full timing chain set (chains, guides, tensioners, VVT sprockets), front engine strip-down, new gaskets and seals | 80,000–100,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early before any misfire or running issues occur. |

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit of fully rebuilt AM04/AM08 engine with upgraded timing components, new chains, guides, bearings, bench tested, 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty | 100,000+ miles. The only safe route if the engine has already jumped timing or suffered any valve contact. |

problems.recommendation: If your DB9 or V12 Vantage is running perfectly but you hear a rattle on cold start or have passed 80,000 miles, preventative chain replacement at a specialist makes excellent financial sense—it costs roughly a third of a replacement engine. However, once the engine has jumped timing or misfires, internal valve damage is almost certain. On a car worth £25,000–£40,000, specialist replacement is nearly always better value than a dealer repair.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt AM04 & AM08 V12 engine prices for your DB9 or V12 Vantage

problems.h4: Core Plug Corrosion – The Silent Coolant Killer in AJ-V8 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin V8 Vantage (2005–2017, AJ37 & AJ-V8 Gen III/IV), DB7 V12 (pre-2003 models share similar design), Vanquish (2001–2007, AJ-V8 derived)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000–90,000 miles, but highly dependent on coolant maintenance history
problems.rootCause: The original steel core (freeze) plugs corrode from the inside out when coolant is not changed every 3–4 years. A pinhole leak allows coolant to enter the oil system or escape externally. If ignored, sudden massive coolant loss causes rapid overheating and catastrophic engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Full set of brass/copper core plugs, intake manifold removal, coolant system flush, new gaskets. Engine remains in car. | 60,000–100,000 miles. Ideal for early detection with minor external staining but no internal damage. |

| Supply & fit of reconditioned AJ-V8 engine with brass core plugs, new bearings, piston rings, seals, bench tested, 12‑month warranty | 100,000+ miles or if coolant has already mixed with oil. Internal bearing damage is almost guaranteed once coolant enters the lubrication system. |

problems.recommendation: Check your V8 Vantage’s service history. If coolant changes are documented every 3 years, risk is low. At the first sign of coolant staining around the engine block, act immediately: specialist core plug replacement with brass upgrades is a permanent fix for under £2,100. If you’ve already seen milky oil or your engine has overheated, do not waste money on repairs—replace the Aston Martin engine with a reconditioned unit.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for reconditioned AJ37 V8 engines with upgraded brass core plugs

problems.h4: Crankshaft & Main Bearing Failure – The V12 Oil Starvation Risk
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB9 (early 2004–2008, AM04 V12), Vanquish (2001–2007, AM04 derived), DB7 V12 (1999–2003), some Rapide (2010–2013)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 50,000–80,000 miles on early cars; later engines more resilient
problems.rootCause: The early AM04 V12 has an oil pickup design that can allow oil to surge away from the pickup under hard cornering or when oil levels are even slightly low. The result is momentary oil starvation to the main bearings, which then spin, seize, and break the crankshaft.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Engine removal, crankshaft regrind or replacement, new main and big-end bearings, oil pump upgrade, modified oil pickup, new gaskets | 60,000–80,000 miles. Only viable if the crankshaft is not broken and the block is undamaged. Requires a specialist machine shop. |

| Supply & fit of fully rebuilt engine with uprated oil pump, modified pickup, new crankshaft and bearings, bench tested, 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty | 100,000+ miles. The only safe option if the crankshaft has snapped or the block is scored from bearing debris. |

problems.recommendation: This failure gives almost no warning—a sudden knock followed by engine seizure. There is no minor fix. On a DB9 worth £20,000–£30,000, specialist replacement with an upgraded oil system is the most cost-effective route. Dealer pricing pushes close to the car’s value, making specialist supply & fit the clear choice. If you’re buying a used AM04-engined car, have an oil pressure test done before purchase.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt AM04 V12 engine prices with uprated oil pumps for your DB9 or Vanquish

problems.h4: Variable Valve Timing (VVT) Sprocket Failure – The V8 Rattle & Poor Idle
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin V8 Vantage (2009–2017, AJ-V8 Gen IV), DB9 (2008–2016, AM08 V12 also affected but less common), Rapide V8 (2010–2020, AJ-V8 Gen IV)
problems.rootCause: The VVT sprockets (camshaft phasers) wear internally, losing their ability to hold oil pressure. This causes excessive clearance, resulting in a pronounced rattle at idle and cold start, reduced low-end torque, and eventually a check engine light with timing correlation codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace all four VVT sprockets, new cam bolts, gaskets, and oil control valves. No chain replacement unless worn. | 60,000–80,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable when rattle is present but no chain stretch or misfires. |

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | VVT sprockets plus full timing chain set, guides, tensioners, oil pump, and water pump while access is open | 80,000–120,000 miles. Best-value long-term solution on higher-mileage engines. |

| Supply & fit of reconditioned engine with new VVT components, timing gear, bearings, and 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty | 120,000+ miles or if the engine has jumped timing and caused valve contact. |

problems.recommendation: If your V8 Vantage rattles for 1–2 seconds on cold start then quiets down, replace the VVT sprockets preventatively at a specialist. However, if the rattle is constant or you have codes P0011/P0021, the chain may also be stretched. We recommend the full VVT+timing kit specialist route on most cars over 90,000 miles—it adds only £800–£1,100 but doubles the life expectancy of the repair.
problems.cta: → Request quotes for AJ-V8 Gen IV engine repairs and replacement engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin
finalCta.paragraph: Aston Martin engine failure is never a small bill, but you have more options than a main dealer rebuild or scrapping the car. Independent Aston Martin engine specialists across the UK supply fully rebuilt V8 and V12 units with upgraded components—brass core plugs, uprated oil pumps, and improved timing chain guides—for 40–60% less than main dealer pricing. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, with nationwide collection, delivery, and supply & fit options. Compare prices from trusted UK specialists and get your Aston Martin back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates (£180–£250/hour) and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network of independent Aston Martin engine specialists (£65–£95/hour labour). Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Aston Martin Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Aston Martin engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Aston Martin Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £3,500 – £9,000 (depending on engine code)
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Aston Martin engine prices

types.title: Aston Martin Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as uprated V12 liner seals and revised AJ-V8 timing chain components – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.
types.cta: Get rebuilt Aston Martin engine quotes

types.title: Used Aston Martin Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available.
types.cta: Check used Aston Martin engine availability

types.title: Aston Martin Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models or owners wanting a zero‑mile engine.
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Aston Martin engine costs

types.title: Aston Martin Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive models where a full recon isn't economical.
types.cta: View refurbished Aston Martin engine options

types.title: Aston Martin Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Aston Martin specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £800 – £1,800 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Aston Martin engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Aston Martin model today.

engineSizes.h2: Aston Martin Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Aston Martin engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Aston Martin 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0‑litre twin‑turbocharged V8 (Mercedes‑AMG M177 derived) entered Aston Martin production with the 2018 Vantage, marking a shift toward smaller‑capacity forced‑induction power. Aston Martin calibrates this engine with its own ECU mapping, exhaust tuning, and dry‑sump lubrication. It has since spread across the modern range, delivering substantially more torque and efficiency than the naturally aspirated 4.3L and 4.7L V8 units it replaced. The primary replacement driver is cooling system and turbocharger assembly failure, though the engine block itself is robust
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin 4.0L petrol engine prices
engineCodes: M177 (Aston Martin variant AE31 for DB11 V8 applications)

items.title: Aston Martin 4.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.3‑litre V8 (AJ‑V8 family) debuted in the 2005 V8 Vantage, Aston Martin’s most successful modern sports car. This naturally aspirated, quad‑cam, 32‑valve engine produced 283kW (380bhp) and 410Nm, revving to 7,000rpm. It was widely praised for its responsive character and exhaust note. The engine is essentially a Jaguar AJ‑V8 Gen III heavily reworked by Aston with a unique cylinder head, inlet manifold, and dry‑sump oil system. Replacement demand is driven by core plug corrosion and VVT sprocket wear on higher‑mileage units
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin 4.3L petrol engine prices
engineCodes: rarely labelled as AJ33 in parts catalogues
commonFailurePoints: Steel core plug corrosion (coolant loss into oil)

items.title: Aston Martin 4.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: A 200‑cc increase over the 4.3L gave the 4.7‑litre AJ‑V8 Gen IV engine improved torque and power (313kW/420bhp). Introduced in the 2009 model‑year V8 Vantage and continuing through 2017, this engine added knock sensors and revised cylinder heads. It also powered the 2010–2020 Rapide S as the sole V8 option. The 4.7L is considered more reliable than the earlier 4.3L, though core plug corrosion remains a long‑term concern. Many high‑mileage units are now entering the replacement market
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin 4.7L petrol engine prices
engineCodes: sometimes listed as AJ133 in parts data
commonFailurePoints: Core plug corrosion (less common than 4.3L but still occurs)

items.title: Aston Martin 5.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: Aston Martin’s first twin‑turbocharged V12, the AE31 5.2‑litre unit, debuted in the 2016 DB11. It produces 447kW (600bhp) and 700Nm, with a higher‑output version (470kW/630bhp) in the DBS Superleggera (2018–2023) and a 759bhp variant in the DBS 770 Ultimate. The 5.2L V12 is a clean‑sheet design with a dry sump, twin‑scroll turbos mounted in the vee, and cylinder‑on‑demand technology. Replacement engines are rare but expensive; most failures relate to turbocharger actuators, oil lines, and early ECU calibration issues
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin 5.2L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: high‑pressure fuel pump issues on early cars

items.title: Aston Martin 5.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The original Aston Martin V8, a 5,340cc V8 designed by Tadek Marek, first appeared in the 1969 DBS V8 and continued in various forms until 1989 in the V8 Vantage (classic “V8 Vantage” model). This engine used Bosch mechanical fuel injection (later variants) and produced between 280bhp (early emissions‑controlled cars) and up to 390bhp in the later “580X” V8 Vantage specification. Replacement engines are now specialist‑rebuild territory, with parts becoming increasingly rare
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin 5.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Aston Martin 5.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5,935cc 60° V12 (AM04/AM08) is Aston Martin’s most famous modern engine. Introduced in the 1999 DB7 Vantage and continuing through the DB9, DBS, Vanquish, Rapide, and V12 Vantage until 2017. This naturally aspirated engine was produced in multiple power outputs from 420bhp to 580bhp. The AM08 (later DB9/DBS) featured a revised block, crank, and variable intake cam timing. Replacement demand is very high due to timing chain guide failure, core plug corrosion (V12 block), and crankshaft bearing issues on early AM04 units. Specialist rebuilt units are widely available
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin 5.9L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: oil pump failure on early AM04 engines

items.title: Aston Martin 6.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.5‑litre naturally aspirated Cosworth‑built V12 is exclusive to the Aston Martin Valkyrie hypercar (2021–present). This 11,100rpm engine produces 1,000bhp from 6.5 litres without forced induction — an engineering masterpiece. The Valkyrie also includes a 140bhp hybrid system (KERS) from Rimac. Replacement engines are essentially unobtainable outside Aston Martin’s hypercar service network. Only 150 road‑legal Valkyries were built
items.cta: Enquire about Aston Martin 6.5L Valkyrie engine availability
commonFailurePoints: any failure handled directly by Aston Martin/Cosworth)

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Aston Martin needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Aston Martin specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Aston Martin Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Aston Martin engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Aston Martin specialists.

entries.title: AM04 5.9L Petrol
entries.description: V12 The AM04 is the original Cosworth-derived V12 fitted to early DB9 (2004–2008) and first-generation V12 Vantage (2009–2012), producing 450 bhp. The most common catastrophic failure is coolant leaking past degraded wet cylinder liner seals, causing oil emulsification and eventual bearing seizure. Our rebuilt AM04 units include upgraded Viton liner seals, new timing chain guides, and a fully inspected crankshaft with new main bearings.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM04 Aston Martin V12 engine replacement

entries.title: AM08 5.9L Petrol
entries.description: V12 The AM08 replaced the AM04 in later DB9 (2008–2016), DBS (2007–2012), Rapide (2010–2018), Vanquish (2012–2018), and V12 Vantage (2009–2018), with outputs from 470–565 bhp. The same liner seal failure plagues the AM08, often with no warning until catastrophic oil contamination occurs. Rebuilt AM08 engines from our network include uprated liner seals, new timing chains and tensioners, and a revised oil pump for improved cold-start protection.
entries.cta: Compare AM08 Aston Martin V12 engine prices

entries.title: M177 4.0L Petrol
entries.description: V8 Biturbo The M177 powers DB11 V8 (2016–2023), Vantage (2018–present), and DBS Superleggera (2018–2023), producing 503–542 bhp. Carbon buildup on intake valves from direct injection is the primary driveability complaint, while infrequent oil changes can lead to turbocharger coking. Our rebuilt M177 units include professionally decarbonised cylinder heads, new turbo oil feed lines, and upgraded PCV baffling to reduce future carbon accumulation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M177 Aston Martin V8 engine replacement

entries.title: AE31 6.2L Petrol
entries.description: V12 The AE31 is a Mercedes-AMG-derived 6.2L V12 fitted to DB11 AMR (2019–2023), DBS 770 Ultimate (2023), and the Cosworth‑derived Valkyrie (2021–present), producing up to 630 bhp. Timing chain stretch and cooling system leaks are the most common high‑mileage failure points. Our rebuilt AE31 units include new timing chains, guides, and tensioners, plus a comprehensively overhauled cooling system with upgraded water pump seals.
entries.cta: Compare AE31 Aston Martin V12 engine prices

entries.title: AJ-V8 Gen III 4.3L Petrol
entries.description: V8 The 4.3L AJ-V8 Gen III (380 bhp) was fitted to early V8 Vantage (2005–2008). The most common failure is camshaft lobe wear caused by oil sludge buildup in the VVT actuators, leading to misfires and failed emissions tests. Rebuilt units from our specialist network include new camshafts, uprated VVT actuators, a full timing chain kit, and an engine flush with revised oil jets.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4.3L AJ-V8 Aston Martin engine replacement

entries.title: AJ-V8 Gen IV 4.7L Petrol
entries.description: V8 The 4.7L AJ-V8 Gen IV (420–430 bhp) replaced the 4.3L in V8 Vantage (2008–2018) and V8 Vantage S. The same camshaft wear issue persists, worsened by extended oil change intervals under previous ownership. Our rebuilt 4.7L units include new camshafts, upgraded VVT components, a revised oil pump, and a recalibrated ECU map for improved reliability.

entries.title: DBA/DBD 4.0L Petrol
entries.description: Straight-Six The 4.0L straight-six (DBA/DBD codes, 240 bhp) powered DB5, DB6, and early DBS models (1963–1972). Hardened liner seals and dropped valve seats are the primary reasons owners seek full rebuilds today. Our network's rebuilt DBA/DBD units include high-quality Viton liner seals, new valve seats and guides, and a dynamically balanced rotating assembly.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4.0L DBA/DBD Aston Martin heritage engine rebuild

entries.title: AEC 5.3L Petrol
entries.description: V8 The 5.3L V8 (AEC code, 310–400 bhp) was fitted to V8 Vantage (1977–1989), Virage (1989–2000), and Lagonda (late 1970s–1980s). Failed liner seals, carburettor/fuel injection age, and worn camshafts drive most replacement enquiries. Rebuilt AEC units include uprated liner seals, fully rebuilt cylinder heads, and a comprehensively serviced fuel system. Closing line (Block A): Can't find your engine code listed above? engineYears.h2: Aston Martin Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When

years.preview: DB7 Inline-Six: The Ford-Sourced Supercharged Era Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The DB7's launch in 1994 introduced the first modern, volume-production Aston Martin to use a Ford-derived platform engine - a Cosworth-developed supercharged 3.2-litre inline-six that enabled the brand to reach a new price point without compromising character.
keyChanges: Cosworth-developed 3.2-litre inline-six replaces ageing Tadek Marek V8 across the DB7 range
Eaton M90 supercharger fitted as standard; intercooled for consistent performance
Ford Mondeo platform architecture underpins the DB7 body structure
Entry-level GT pricing broadens Aston Martin's buyer base for the first time
mainEngines: Cosworth AJ6 3.2 Supercharged - 3.2-litre supercharged inline-6 (Aston Martin DB7 Coupe, DB7 Volante)
knownFor: Supercharger whine gives a distinctive character unlike any previous Aston; immediately recognisable to enthusiasts
Cosworth-tuned heads deliver strong mid-range torque but the block is fundamentally Ford-derived - parts sourcing is now specialist territory
Supercharger nose-seal wear and intercooler hose perishing are the most common high-mileage faults
Cylinder head gasket failures observed on hard-driven examples above 80,000 miles
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Aston Martin DB7 3.2 engine replacement - Aston Martin DB7 supercharged inline-six rebuild - Aston Martin 1994 engine for sale - Aston Martin DB7 Cosworth engine UK
years.cta: Compare prices for 1994–1998 Aston Martin DB7 replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: AML V12 Debut: The 5.9-Litre V12 Arrives Major Engine Family Introduction Performance Landmark
years.description: The 1999 DB7 Vantage marked a transformational moment for the brand - the debut of the bespoke AML 5.9-litre V12, developed in partnership with Cosworth, establishing the engine architecture that would underpin every flagship Aston Martin for the next two decades.
keyChanges: All-new 5.9-litre 60° V12 (AM2) replaces the inline-six in the DB7 Vantage
Engine co-developed by Ford Advanced Powertrain and Cosworth; not derived from any existing production unit
Deep-skirt aluminium block, six-bolt mains, and dual overhead camshafts per bank - a clean-sheet design
DB7 Vantage enters production at Geneva Motor Show 1999; V12 immediately defines the modern Aston engine identity
mainEngines: AML V12 AM2 - 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12 (Aston Martin DB7 Vantage, DB7 GT, DB7 GTA)
knownFor: Linear, high-revving power delivery with exceptional acoustic refinement - widely regarded as one of the great naturally aspirated engines of its era
Robust architecture with a minimal failure rate when properly maintained with correct 5W-40 synthetic oil
Timing chain tensioner sensitivity to extended oil change intervals - the AML V12 is unforgiving of neglect in this area
Early AM2 units use individual throttle bodies; composite plenums introduced on later variants for improved part-load efficiency
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Aston Martin V12 5.9 engine replacement - Aston Martin DB7 Vantage V12 engine rebuild UK - Aston Martin 1999 V12 engine for sale
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999–2000 Aston Martin V12 replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: Vanquish V12 and the Gaydon Platform Transition Performance Landmark
years.description: The 2001 V12 Vanquish launched the new Gaydon era of Aston Martin, introducing an uprated 5.9-litre V12 in the AM03 specification - a bespoke bonded aluminium VH architecture platform that would form the foundation of every significant Aston model through to 2018.
keyChanges: AM03 V12 introduced in the Vanquish with revised intake and uprated power over AM2
New VH (vertical/horizontal) bonded aluminium platform debuts - significantly stiffer and lighter than DB7 structure
Cosworth manufacturing partnership continues; Cologne engine plant operational from 2004
DB9 development underway; AM04 engine code prepared for the next-generation grand tourer
mainEngines: AML V12 AM03 - 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12, 460 bhp (Aston Martin V12 Vanquish)
popularModels: Aston Martin DB7 GT (continued production, AM2A unit)
knownFor: AM03 is regarded as the most sonically pure of the early V12 specifications - individual throttle bodies give an instantaneous throttle response
VH platform aluminium structure requires specialist repair expertise; standard body shops cannot work on these cars
Vanquish paddle-shift gearbox of this era has a notorious reputation for slow, jerky changes - the engine itself is blameless
Cosworth-built quality is exceptionally high; block distortion failures are essentially unheard of on properly serviced examples
years.cta: Find 2001–2003 Aston Martin Vanquish V12 replacement engine prices

years.preview: DB9 Launch and the AM04/AM11 V12 Family Establishment Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The DB9's arrival in 2004 introduced the AM04 and AM11 engine codes - the production-refined evolution of the AML V12 that would become the most prolific Aston Martin engine in history, built at the brand's dedicated Cologne facility and fitted across the entire model range for the next twelve years.
keyChanges: AM04 and AM11 5.9-litre V12 enter production for DB9 - Cologne engine plant now operational
Composite intake plenum replaces individual throttle bodies on AM04/AM11; improved part-load efficiency
Euro 3 emissions compliance achieved across the range
Vanquish S launches with AM06 uprated to 520 bhp - highest output Aston in production at launch
Dry-sump lubrication system standard across all V12 variants; oil consumption very low in good health
mainEngines: AML V12 AM04 - 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12, 450 bhp (Aston Martin DB9)
AML V12 AM06 - 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12, 520 bhp (Aston Martin V12 Vanquish S)
AML V12 AM11 - 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12, 450–470 bhp (DB9, DBS)
knownFor: AM11 is widely regarded as the definitive expression of the naturally aspirated Aston V12 - smooth, linear, and long-lived
Valve cover gasket seepage typically observed after 60,000 miles due to heat cycling; inexpensive fix but commonly neglected
Cam phaser wear accelerated by infrequent oil changes - 12,500-mile intervals must be adhered to strictly
DB9 of this era is the most affordable entry point into the VH platform and the AM11 V12 - high demand for replacement units on the used market
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Aston Martin DB9 V12 engine replacement - Aston Martin AM11 engine rebuild - Aston Martin 2004 V12 engine for sale - Aston Martin Vanquish S engine UK
years.cta: Compare 2004–2006 Aston Martin DB9 and Vanquish V12 engine quotes

years.preview: DBS, Vantage V12 and Euro 4/5 Transition Major Reliability Revision
years.description: This period saw the AM11 V12 refined to Euro 4 and Euro 5 compliance, cylinder deactivation introduced on later units, and the V8 Vantage debut - the first time a separate, lower-displacement engine family had been offered in a modern Aston Martin.
keyChanges: DBS launched with uprated AM08 V12 - 510 bhp, revised cams and composite intake
AM11 Mark II introduced with composite intake and cylinder deactivation technology (2007)
V8 Vantage debuts with 4.3-litre Jaguar-derived AJ-V8 in 2005, uprated to 4.7-litre AJ41 by 2008
Euro 4 compliance across the range by 2007; Euro 5 from 2009
Rapide four-door sports saloon enters development; AM11 confirmed as its powerplant
One-77 hypercar project launched with bespoke 7.3-litre Cosworth-developed V12 (AM77)
mainEngines: AML V12 AM08 - 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12, 510 bhp (Aston Martin DBS)
AML V12 AM11 Mk II - 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12, 470 bhp with cylinder deactivation (DB9)
Jaguar AJ-V8 4.3 / AJ41 4.7 - 4.3/4.7-litre naturally aspirated V8 (Aston Martin V8 Vantage)
AML V12 AM77 - 7.3-litre Cosworth V12 (Aston Martin One-77, limited production)
knownFor: DBS AM08 is a high-water mark for the naturally aspirated AML V12 - 510 bhp with analogue, linear delivery and extraordinary soundtrack
Cylinder deactivation solenoid faults on Euro 5 AM11 units can cause rough idle and MIL illumination - a known issue on post-2009 DB9 cars
AJ41 4.7-litre V8 Vantage is regarded as one of the most reliable Aston Martin powerplants ever produced; robust Jaguar-derived architecture
AJ-V8 4.3 early units can suffer throttle body sensor faults and coolant crossover pipe failures at high mileage
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Aston Martin DBS V12 engine replacement - Aston Martin V8 Vantage 4.7 engine rebuild - Aston Martin AM11 cylinder deactivation fault - Aston Martin 2009 V12 engine for sale UK
years.cta: Search 2007–2011 Aston Martin DBS and Vantage replacement engine prices

years.preview: New Vanquish, AM28/AM29 and Euro 5 Consolidation
years.description: The second-generation Vanquish of 2012 brought the AM11 V12 to its most powerful naturally aspirated specification - 565 bhp in the AM11 Vanquish - while the broader range consolidated on Euro 5-compliant versions of the established V8 and V12 families ahead of the twin-turbo era.
keyChanges: New Vanquish launches with AM11 uprated to 565 bhp - 6.0-litre specification with revised camshafts and higher compression
AM28 and AM29 codes introduced for Vanquish and Rapide S applications
V8 Vantage S updated with revised AJ41 producing 436 bhp
Virage model introduced (2011–2012) then discontinued; AM11 unit used throughout
Euro 5 compliance maintained across the full range; no diesel offered at any point in Aston Martin history
mainEngines: AML V12 AM11 6.0 - 6.0-litre naturally aspirated V12, 565 bhp (Aston Martin Vanquish)
AML V12 AM28 - 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12 (Vanquish, Rapide)
AML V12 AM29 - 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12, uprated (Vanquish S, Rapide S)
Jaguar AJ41 4.7 - 4.7-litre naturally aspirated V8 (V8 Vantage S)
knownFor: 6.0-litre AM11 Vanquish specification is widely considered the finest development of the naturally aspirated AML V12 - an engine of genuine engineering legacy
Timing chain tensioner fatigue is a known long-term concern on high-mileage examples above 100,000 miles; cold-start rattle warrants immediate investigation
Rapide S AM29 suffers from rear-section exhaust corrosion in UK climate conditions - not an engine fault but affects ownership cost
This generation represents peak demand for AM11 V12 replacement units; highest search volume of any Aston engine family on EngineMarket
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Aston Martin Vanquish V12 engine replacement - Aston Martin AM28 engine UK - Aston Martin Rapide S engine rebuild - Aston Martin 2013 V12 engine for sale - Aston Martin 2014 Vanquish engine quotes
years.cta: Get 2012–2015 Aston Martin Vanquish and Rapide engine replacement quotes instantly

years.preview: DB11 Launch: Twin-Turbo V12 and the AE31 Era Major Engine Family Introduction Technology Introduction
years.description: The DB11's 2016 debut replaced the long-serving AM11 with an entirely new 5.2-litre twin-turbocharged V12 (AE31), marking Aston Martin's first forced-induction V12 and the most significant powertrain change in the brand's modern history.
keyChanges: New 5.2-litre twin-turbo V12 (AE31) replaces 5.9/6.0-litre naturally aspirated AM11 in the flagship model
AE31 produces 600 bhp - turbocharged torque delivery is a fundamental character shift from the AM11
Hot-V twin-turbo layout with direct injection; Euro 6 compliant from launch
DB11 V12 is the first Aston Martin built on the new bonded aluminium/composite NAMT platform
Partnership with Mercedes-AMG formalised - AMG electrical architecture and infotainment integrated into DB11
mainEngines: AML V12 AE31 5.2 TT - 5.2-litre twin-turbocharged V12, 600 bhp (Aston Martin DB11 V12)
popularModels: Aston Martin Vanquish S (continued on AM29, naturally aspirated)
knownFor: AE31 delivers substantially more low-end torque than the AM11 - a more effortless grand tourer but a different character entirely to the old naturally aspirated unit
Direct injection introduces intake valve carbon buildup risk on high-mileage examples - walnut blasting recommended above 80,000 miles
Early DB11 V12 units reported some turbocharger wastegate rattle; addressed by revised calibration in later builds
Exhaust active valve system gives variable acoustic character; valve actuator failures are an emerging fault on early cars
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Aston Martin DB11 V12 engine replacement - Aston Martin 5.2 twin turbo engine rebuild - Aston Martin AE31 engine UK - Aston Martin 2016 engine for sale
years.cta: Compare 2016–2017 Aston Martin DB11 V12 replacement engine prices

years.preview: AMG V8 Integration: M177 Across the Range Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The 2017 DB11 V8 introduced the Mercedes-AMG M177 4.0-litre twin-turbo V8 to Aston Martin, beginning a new powertrain partnership that would eventually see the AMG-sourced V8 become the brand's most widely deployed engine family across the DB11, Vantage, DBS Superleggera, and DBX.
keyChanges: AMG M177 4.0-litre twin-turbo V8 introduced in DB11 V8 (2017); first AMG-sourced engine in production Aston Martin
Aston Martin Vantage (2018) launches with M177, replacing AJ41 V8 - a generational shift for the entry model
DBS Superleggera (2018) launches with uprated AE31 V12 and M178 V8 option
DBX SUV (2020) debuts with M177 V8 - first Aston Martin SUV in production history
Euro 6d-TEMP compliance across all new models from 2019
Revised ECU mapping and bespoke exhaust tuning differentiate Aston V8 from AMG GT spec
mainEngines: AMG M177 4.0 TT - 4.0-litre twin-turbo V8, 503–510 bhp (DB11 V8, Vantage, DBX)
AMG M178 4.0 TT - 4.0-litre twin-turbo V8, 528+ bhp (DBS Superleggera, DBX707)
AML V12 AE31 - 5.2-litre twin-turbo V12, 600–630 bhp (DB11 V12, DBS)
knownFor: AMG M177/M178 hot-V twin-turbo layout is an extremely strong architecture; Aston Martin's specific ECU calibration and exhaust tuning give a distinct character from AMG GT variants
Intake valve carbon buildup is the most common long-term maintenance item on direct injection M177/M178 units - symptoms include rough idle and loss of power from 70,000 miles
Early 2018–2019 Vantage M177 units reported active engine mount rattle; resolved by Aston Martin service bulletin SIB-2019-044
DBX M177 is the highest-volume Aston Martin ever produced; replacement engine demand is growing rapidly as early examples reach high mileage
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Aston Martin Vantage 4.0 V8 engine replacement - Aston Martin DBX engine rebuild UK - Aston Martin M177 engine for sale - Aston Martin DBS Superleggera V12 engine - Aston Martin 2019 V8 engine quotes
years.cta: Search 2017–2022 Aston Martin V8 and V12 replacement engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: DB12, Valhalla PHEV and the Hybrid Era Technology Introduction
years.description: The DB12's 2023 launch signalled a step-change in AMG V8 output to 680 bhp, while the Valhalla introduced Aston Martin's first plug-in hybrid system - a bespoke flat-plane-crank 4.0-litre V8 paired with three electric motors - marking the brand's definitive entry into electrified performance engineering.
keyChanges: DB12 launches with uprated M178 4.0-litre twin-turbo V8 producing 680 bhp - highest output V8 in a standard production Aston Martin at launch
Aston Martin Valhalla PHEV enters production (2025) with bespoke flat-plane-crank 4.0-litre twin-turbo V8 producing 817 bhp, plus three electric motors for 1,064 bhp combined output
Valhalla is Aston Martin's first mid-engine production car and first PHEV
AE31 V12 continues in Vanquish (2024) with uprated block, larger turbos, new camshafts and cylinder heads - highest output AML V12 ever in a road car
Euro 6d and WLTP compliance across all new models
St Athan facility operational for EV/PHEV integration and battery pack assembly
mainEngines: AMG M178 4.0 TT (DB12 spec) - 4.0-litre twin-turbo V8, 680 bhp (Aston Martin DB12)
Bespoke AM FPC V8 4.0 PHEV - 4.0-litre flat-plane-crank twin-turbo V8, 817 bhp + 3x eMotors, 1,064 bhp combined (Aston Martin Valhalla)
AML V12 AE31 Gen3 - 5.2-litre twin-turbo V12, uprated (Aston Martin Vanquish 2024)
knownFor: DB12 M178 in 680 bhp specification is the most powerful front-engine Aston Martin grand tourer ever produced - a substantial performance step over the DB11
Valhalla PHEV architecture is genuinely bespoke - the flat-plane-crank V8 shares no architecture with the AMG-sourced M177/M178 units
Replacement and rebuild infrastructure for PHEV components is exclusively authorised-specialist territory; independent workshops currently lack tooling for Valhalla high-voltage systems
Long-term reliability data on Valhalla PHEV powertrain is limited given recency of production - early ownership data emerging through 2025
years.cta: Explore 2023–2025 Aston Martin DB12 and Valhalla engine options and get specialist quotes

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Aston Martin was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Aston Martin specialists.

faq.h2: Aston Martin Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Aston Martin owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Aston Martin engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Aston Martin engine typically costs between £5,500 and £9,500 for the 4.3L or 4.7L V8, rising to £7,500–£12,500 for a reconditioned 5.9L V12 (AM04/AM08). A used, lower‑mileage unit can start from £3,000–£5,000 but carries higher risk. Supply‑and‑fit adds £1,200–£2,000 depending on model complexity. Main dealer pricing for a new V12 engine can exceed £25,000 — which is why most owners choose a specialist‑rebuilt unit.
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What affects the cost of a Aston Martin engine replacement?
items.answer: Four main factors: engine size (V12 costs significantly more than V8), condition grade (rebuilt with upgraded internals vs used as‑is), vehicle age (DB9 and Vantage are straightforward; later DB11 electronics add labour), and supply‑only vs supply‑and‑fit (fitting typically adds £1,200–£2,000). Location also matters — London workshops can charge 20–30% more than Midlands specialists. Our free comparison tool shows transparent, all‑in quotes from vetted Aston Martin engine specialists across the UK.
items.cta: Get accurate Aston Martin engine supply and fit quotes

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Aston Martin?
items.answer: In most cases, yes — especially if the chassis, gearbox, and bodywork are sound. A specialist‑rebuilt Aston Martin engine with upgraded timing components, brass core plugs, and a new oil pump can outlast the original unit by 50,000+ miles. The alternative — selling the car with a failed engine — often means accepting 50–70% of market value. If your DB9 or V8 Vantage is otherwise well‑maintained and worth £20,000+, replacement makes strong financial sense.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt engine vs replacement costs for your Aston Martin

items.question: What's included in a Aston Martin engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply‑and‑fit package includes: sourcing the correct rebuilt or reconditioned engine (by engine code), removal of the failed unit, installation of the replacement, all new gaskets, seals, fluids and filters, a workmanship warranty (typically 12 months), disposal of the old engine, and a post‑installation road test. Fitting labour usually adds £1,200–£2,000. Always ask whether the quote includes delivery of the replacement engine to the workshop.
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin engine supply and fit packages

items.question: How long does a Aston Martin engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common models like the DB9 or V8 Vantage, a like‑for‑like engine swap typically takes 3–5 working days. More complex models (DB11, DBS Superleggera with twin‑turbo V12) can take 5–7 days due to additional ancillaries and electronic calibration. The biggest variable is engine availability — our network of specialist suppliers stocks commonly replaced codes (AM04, AM08, AJ33, AJ41, M177) for rapid dispatch. Use our tool to get realistic lead times from multiple specialists.
items.cta: Check Aston Martin engine replacement turnaround times

items.question: Do reconditioned Aston Martin engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes — every reconditioned or rebuilt Aston Martin engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Many specialists offer extended 24‑month coverage on rebuilt units with upgraded internal components. Standard warranty covers major internals: cylinder block, crankshaft, pistons, connecting rods, timing chain, oil pump, and cylinder head. Always confirm exact terms — some warranties exclude fitting labour or require an approved workshop for installation.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Aston Martin engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Aston Martin engine?
items.answer: Used engines are cheaper upfront (£3,000–£5,000) and suit a temporary fix or if you plan to sell the car within 12–18 months. However, you inherit the original failure risks (timing chain wear, core plug corrosion). Reconditioned engines (£5,500–£12,500) include new bearings, piston rings, seals, and upgraded components that address the original design flaws. For a long‑term keeper, reconditioned with a 12‑month+ warranty is almost always better value.
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Aston Martin engine prices

items.question: Where can I find Aston Martin engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK‑wide network of independent Aston Martin engine specialists — no need to search locally. Known specialist hubs include the Midlands (Cheshire, Derby), London, and Manchester, but we also cover Scotland, Wales, and the South West. Enter your registration or select your model and engine code, and we'll match you with vetted suppliers who stock your specific engine and can offer supply‑only or supply‑and‑fit. Nationwide delivery and collection are available.
items.cta: Find Aston Martin engine specialists UK – get instant quotes

items.question: What are the most common Aston Martin engine failures?
items.answer: The four most common failures that drive replacement demand: timing chain guide embrittlement on AM08 5.9L V12 (DB9, DBS, V12 Vantage — 70k–110k miles), core plug corrosion on AJ‑V8 4.3L and 4.7L engines (V8 Vantage — 60k–90k miles), crankshaft bearing seizure on early AM04 V12s (2004–2008 DB9 — 50k–80k miles), and water pump/turbo coolant line failures on M177 4.0L twin‑turbo V8 (modern Vantage, DB11 V8). Each of these can lead to catastrophic engine failure requiring full replacement rather than repair.
items.cta: Read about common Aston Martin engine problems – then compare replacement prices

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Aston Martin engine?
items.answer: Compare at least three quotes — prices for the same engine code can vary by £1,500+ between suppliers. For tight budgets, a used engine with a short warranty (30–90 days) keeps costs down. For long‑term ownership, invest in a reconditioned unit with upgraded components (brass core plugs, uprated oil pump, revised timing guides). Ask each supplier whether fitting, delivery, and VAT are included in the quoted price. Supply‑only can save £500–£1,000 if you have a trusted independent garage for installation.
items.cta: Compare the best price on Aston Martin engines – free instant quotes
